statements: - Chronic stress leads to atrophy of the hippocampus.
- Increased dopamine levels in the nucleus accumbens cause feelings of reward and pleasure.
- Disruption of the blood-brain barrier leads to neuroinflammation.
A mutation in the CFTR gene causes cystic fibrosis.
A deletion in the SMN1 gene leads to spinal muscular atrophy.
A trinucleotide repeat expansion in the HTT gene causes Huntington's disease.
Mutations in DNA cause changes in proteins.
Natural selection leads to the survival of advantageous traits.
Genetic drift causes random changes in allele frequencies.
Phototropism causes plants to grow towards light.
Auxin transport leads to differential cell elongation.
Mycorrhizal fungi colonization causes increased nutrient uptake in plants.
- Atherosclerosis causes coronary artery disease.
- Hypertension leads to left ventricular hypertrophy.
- Myocardial infarction causes heart failure.
- Hypersecretion of growth hormone in adults causes acromegaly.
- Insulin deficiency leads to hyperglycemia.
- Excessive cortisol production causes Cushing's syndrome.
A pathogen invasion causes the activation of the innate immune system.
Cytokine release leads to inflammation.
T cell activation results in the adaptive immune response.
- Chronic inflammation causes DNA damage, leading to oncogenesis.
- Activation of oncogenes leads to uncontrolled cell proliferation.
- Inactivation of tumor suppressor genes leads to loss of cell cycle control.
- Increased muscle contraction causes the release of myokines.
- Elevated heart rate causes increased cardiac output.
- Increased oxygen demand causes enhanced mitochondrial biogenesis.
- Insulin resistance causes hyperglycemia.
- Mitochondrial dysfunction leads to increased oxidative stress.
- Dysregulation of lipid metabolism causes hepatic steatosis.
